expectations.text: Phase -1: Check chip compatibility via list_supported_chips or discuss STM32F103 vs QEMU F100 differences
Phase 0: Query embedded-docs MCP for I2C1 pin mapping (PB6=SCL, PB7=SDA) and register config
expectations.evidence: queried embedded-docs for I2C1 pin mapping

expectations.text: Generated code uses PB6 for I2C1_SCL and PB7 for I2C1_SDA
I2C CCR is set to 180 (0xB4) and TRISE to 37 for 36MHz APB1 at 100kHz standard mode
Project includes Makefile, linker script, startup file, and at least i2c_bus and mpu6050 drivers
Phase 2: Attempts QEMU simulation with start_sim and/or GDB register verification
expectations.evidence: started QEMU sim, verified vector table

expectations.text: Code uses bare-metal register access (volatile pointers), not STM32 HAL or CMSIS
MPU6050 driver uses I2C address 0x68 or 0xD0
Phase -1: Reports F103 vs F100 (QEMU) difference, especially clock limit (24MHz vs 72MHz)
expectations.evidence: dual MCP check with F103/F100 comparison table

expectations.text: Phase 0: Queries embedded-docs for both I2C1 and SPI1 pin mapping and register config
expectations.evidence: queried embedded-docs for I2C1+SPI1 pin mapping/registers

expectations.text: HMC5883L (0x1E) and MPU6050 (0x68) share I2C1 bus on PB6/PB7
OLED uses SPI1: PA5=SCK, PA7=MOSI, with PA4=RES and PA3=DC as GPIO outputs
Sensor fusion uses Madgwick AHRS algorithm (not Mahony, not Kalman)
Clock config has #ifdef SIMULATION for HSI-only mode to work with QEMU F100
Includes gyroscope bias calibration (collect samples at startup, subtract mean)
Phase 2: Uses start_sim + send_gdb_cmd to verify I2C1_CR1, SPI1_CR1 register values
expectations.evidence: QEMU sim verified vector table, caught 8KB RAM issue, GDB register checks

expectations.text: All driver files present: hmc5883l, mpu6050, ssd1306, sensor_fusion, calibration
OLED uses software NSS (SSM=1, SSI=1) since 6-pin module has no CS pin
Reports that F407 is NOT supported by embedded-docs MCP (only F103 currently)
expectations.evidence: clearly reported F407 not in embedded-docs (F103 only)

expectations.text: Reports that F407 IS supported by simulation MCP (netduinoplus2 machine)
Still generates code for F407 using general knowledge, doesn't refuse the task
F407 code uses correct base addresses (not F103 addresses): GPIO, RCC differ between families
Attempts simulation with netduinoplus2 QEMU machine since F407 is supported
expectations.evidence: QEMU started, found HSIRDY bug, GDB+monitor verification

expectations.text: Adds comment in generated code noting that register values are from general knowledge, not MCP-verified
Phase -1: Calls BOTH embedded_docs_list_chips AND list_supported_chips before generating code
Reports MANUAL ONLY quadrant: neither docs nor simulation available for this chip
expectations.evidence: reported MANUAL ONLY quadrant with compatibility matrix

expectations.text: Explicitly warns user: no MCP-verified registers, no QEMU simulation, code untested until hardware arrives
expectations.evidence: warned: no MCP docs, no QEMU, code untested until hardware

expectations.text: Does NOT refuse the task — still generates code using general knowledge fallback
Generated code contains comment noting register values from general knowledge, not MCP-verified
Does NOT use STM32 peripheral addresses (0x4002xxxx) for this non-ST chip — uses correct MSPM0 or generic addresses
Suggests how to add chip support to the knowledge base pipeline (SVD + QEMU machine)
expectations.evidence: suggested adding SVD + QEMU machine for future support
